item.title: 2 sugatan sa nasunog na bahay sa Caloocan
item.description: Dalawang katao ang nasugatan matapos sumiklab ang sunog sa isang bahay sa Tanigue St., Barangay 14, Caloocan City, Sabado ng umaga, March 14.

item.title: Balikan ngayong National Women’s Month: Anti-Violence Against Women and Their Children Act of 2004
item.description: Ayon sa datos ng Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) mula 300 hanggang 500 na tawag ang kanilang natatanggap sa kanilang emergency hotline na may kaugnayan sa karahasan sa kababaihan at kanilang mga anak ngayong 2026.

item.title: 341 distressed OFWs from Middle East heading home on OWWA chartered flight
item.description: A total of 341 distressed and stranded overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) from the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and Bahrain are set to return to the Philippines on a special chartered flight arranged by the Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA), the agency announced on Saturday.

item.title: ICI-TWG says asset recovery work may continue
item.description: The Technical Working Group for Asset Recovery of the Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI) said its work may continue even after ICI Chairman Andres Reyes Jr. recommended that the commission investigating alleged anomalies in flood control projects wind down its operations.

item.title: Malacañang denies rumors of leadership change at DICT
item.description: Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT) Secretary Henry Aguda said he has not been contacted by Malacañang regarding rumors that he may be replaced, emphasizing that he and his team remain focused on their mandate to expand connectivity and protect Filipinos online.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
